en-US.meta_description: Press kit for 40 Below with screenshots, icons, download bundles, and direct promo-code contact information.
en-US.title: 40 Below Press Kit
en-US.lead: Everything you need to cover 40 Below, including screenshots, app icons, and email for promo-code requests.
en-US.overview_outro: This press kit gathers everything you need to cover 40 Below, but if you have any other questions or need anything else, just use the email link at the bottom of this page to shoot me a note. I'll get right back to you. Thanks for taking a look!
en-US.factsheet_intro: A quick App Store-style snapshot for press coverage.

factsheet_store.technology_value: Built with the latest Apple technology

en-US.contact_body: Email {email} if you would like a promo code or have a press question about 40 Below.
en-US.contact_body_prefill: Hi Michael,%0A%0AI'd like a promo code or more information for coverage of 40 Below.%0A%0AThanks!
en-US.download_note: Each zip includes app icons plus a short inventory note. iPad and Mac bundles are ready for future screenshots.
en-US.technology_intro: 40 Below spans the latest Apple frameworks and platform behaviors across iPhone, iPad, and Mac.

technology_cards.title: Weather, maps, and background refresh
body: WeatherKit powers current conditions, forecasts, cached snapshots, and background temperature refreshes in the main app.
Core Location handles current-location lookup and authorization so 40 Below can show local temperature automatically.
MapKit is used for reverse geocoding so raw coordinates become readable city and place names.
BackgroundTasks keeps temperature and cached location data fresh when the app is not foregrounded.

technology_cards.title: Siri, Spotlight, Shortcuts, and Apple Intelligence
body: App Intents powers Siri, Shortcuts, and Spotlight actions for showing the current temperature, speaking it aloud, and opening the forecast.
SnippetIntent and Apple Intelligence-era intent presentation APIs show inline temperature cards from Siri and Spotlight without always bouncing people into the app.
NSUserActivity donation and Core Spotlight indexing help iOS surface temperature, forecast, and share actions proactively in search and system suggestions.

technology_cards.title: Mac-native touches and sharing
body: The Mac app includes menu bar behavior, dock menu integration, multi-window lifecycle support, app activation behavior, and Mac-specific email flow.
MenuBarExtra and NSStatusItem keep 40 Below resident in the macOS menu bar for at-a-glance temperature access.
ServiceManagement and a login helper app support Launch at Login on macOS.
ShareLink, UIActivityViewController, and NSSharingServicePicker provide native Apple sharing across iPhone, iPad, and Mac.

technology_cards.title: SwiftUI at the center
body: SwiftUI drives the core app UI, settings, onboarding, share-card rendering, and most cross-platform presentation.
Localization support uses String(localized:), .lproj bundles, InfoPlist.strings, and Locale-aware formatting across app UI, App Intents, and system-facing strings.

Accessibility uses explicit SwiftUI accessibility labels, hints, and values for temperatures, forecast rows, buttons, and status information, plus adaptations for Reduce Motion and increased contrast.

en-US.gallery_intro: Preview the available press images by platform. iPad and Mac sections are set up now and waiting for approved screenshots.
en-US.empty_state_body: This gallery is wired up and ready for the first approved screenshots.
